title: campanula rosmarinifolia
intro: Campanula rosmarinifolia, also known as sea bell or Rosmarin-leghbaune, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). Traditionally, Campanula rosmarinifolia has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and saponins. The health benefits of Campanula rosmarinifolia include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Campanula rosmarinifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support respiratory health and soothe digestive issues.
